title: sebastiania marginata var. genuina
intro: Sebastiania marginata var. genuina, also known as the West Indian mastich or the false myrrh tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Sebastiania marginata var. genuina include relief from coughs and respiratory issues, reduction of inflammation, and antimicrobial properties that aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's resin is often extracted and used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, and can be found in the form of tinctures, ointments, and salves.
